articles.title: India cuts export duties on petrol, diesel and aviation turbine fuel
articles.description:
India will cut its export duty on petrol diesel and aviation turbine fuel for the fortnight starting June 1, its government said in a statement on Saturday.

articles.title: India Slashes Export Duties on Fuel: A Strategic Move
articles.description: India has announced a reduction in export duties for petrol, diesel, and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) effective from June 1. The export duty on petrol is set at 1.5 rupees per litre, diesel at 13.5 rupees, and ATF at 9.5 rupees per litre. These rates will be reviewed fortnightly based on international crude prices.

articles.title: Oil prices could hit $160 as ExxonMobil shareholders approve move to Texas
articles.description: ExxonMobil VP warns crude oil prices could hit $160 per barrel as oil inventories plummet.

articles.title: India halves petrol export duty; diesel, aviation fuel also slashed from June 1
articles.description: The Centre has further reduced export duties on petrol, diesel and ATF for June 1–15, following a fortnightly review linked to global fuel prices, while leaving domestic fuel taxes unchanged.

articles.title: Government revises windfall tax on fuel exports from June 1
articles.description: New windfall tax rates on fuel exports take effect from June 1, 2026. Taxes are back on diesel and aviation turbine fuel after a period of nil levies. Petrol exports will also attract a duty. These revisions reflect changes in global oil prices and refiner margins. The government reviews these rates fortnightly.

articles.title: Rachel Reeves just dangled a carrot in front of drivers - does she think we're stupid?
articles.description: EXCLUSIVE: Rachel Reeves just made a major change for petrol and diesel owners - but is it all too little too late?

articles.title: Amidst Middle East and Hormuz crises, India purchases 5 million barrels of crude oil; will petrol and diesel prices drop?
articles.description: India's largest state-owned oil company, Indian Oil Corporation (IOC), has purchased approximately 5 million barrels of crude oil from the international market this week. - Amidst Middle East and Hormuz crises, India purchases 5 million barrels of crude oil; will petrol and diesel prices drop?

articles.title: Montek Singh Ahluwalia warns India should prepare for oil above $100 if West Asia conflict drags on
articles.description: Montek Singh Ahluwalia said India should prepare for crude oil prices above $100 per barrel if the West Asia conflict drags on, warning of fuel, fertiliser and helium supply constraints.

articles.title: Higher fuel costs raise red flags across Maine’s economy
articles.description: Ripple effects of high gas prices, soft retail sales and persistent economic uncertainty may make 2026 a tough year financially for many Mainers.
